iDo.problem: Rewrite $-6 - (-2)$ with a single sign (do not evaluate).

solution.note: Look at the two touching signs between the numbers: − and −.
The touching signs are the same (− and −), so rewrite them as a single +.

atomSequence.intro: Two touching signs become one: **same signs → +**, **different signs → −**. Each example below changes just one feature.

items.label: P
items.note: Touching + and − are different → a single −.

items.label: P
items.note: Change the operation to −: now − and − are the same → a single +.

items.label: P
items.note: Change the leading term to negative: the touching signs (+ and −) are unchanged → a single −.

steps: Touching signs − and − are the same.

weDo.problem: Now go the other way: rewrite $4 - 3$ using two signs.
steps: A single − is the same as + (−).

foundation.q: Rewrite as a single sign (do not evaluate): $28 + (-76)$
